questions.questionText: The mechanism of action of Penicillins and Cephalosporins involves:
questions.optionD: Inhibition of bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding to Penicillin-Binding Proteins (PBPs)
questions.explanation: Beta-lactams are bactericidal, disrupting the cross-linking of peptidoglycan.

questions.questionText: Which class of antibiotics inhibits protein synthesis by binding to the 50S ribosomal subunit?
questions.explanation: Macrolides, Clindamycin, and Linezolid bind 50S (buy AT 30, CCEL at 50).

questions.questionText: Aminoglycosides (e.g., Gentamicin) exhibit notable serious toxicities affecting the:
questions.explanation: Irreversible hearing loss and acute tubular necrosis are major risks.

questions.questionText: Which antibiotic classically causes 'Red Man Syndrome' (histamine release) when infused intravenously too rapidly?
questions.explanation: Vancomycin requires slow infusion to prevent this non-allergic histamine release.

questions.questionText: Fluoroquinolones (e.g., Ciprofloxacin) exert their bactericidal effect by inhibiting:
questions.optionB: DNA Gyrase (Topoisomerase II) and Topoisomerase IV
questions.explanation: This prevents DNA supercoiling and uncoiling necessary for replication.

questions.questionText: The combination of Sulfamethoxazole and Trimethoprim (Cotrimoxazole) is deeply synergistic because they:
questions.optionA: Prevent protein synthesis at 30S and 50S subunits
questions.optionB: Increase one another's renal excretion half-life
questions.optionC: Both destroy the cell wall simultaneously
questions.optionD: Sequentially block sequential steps in bacterial folic acid synthesis
questions.explanation: Sulfa drugs inhibit dihydropteroate synthase; Trimethoprim inhibits dihydrofolate reductase.

questions.questionText: Isoniazid (INH), a first-line antitubercular drug, requires co-administration of which vitamin to prevent peripheral neuropathy?
questions.explanation: INH competitively inhibits Vitamin B6 utilization.

questions.questionText: Amphotericin B, a potent systemic antifungal, binds to the fungal cell membrane by interacting with:
questions.explanation: This forms artificial pores that cause lethal leakage of intracellular ions.

questions.questionText: Acyclovir is a guanosine analog specifically useful against Herpes Simplex Virus because it requires activation by the viral enzyme:
questions.explanation: Only infected cells with viral thymidine kinase can efficiently phosphorylate and trap acyclovir.

questions.questionText: Methotrexate acts as a chemotherapeutic agent by competitively inhibiting:
questions.explanation: This deprives the rapidly dividing cell of tetrahydrofolate needed for purine/thymidine synthesis.
